behaviors.description: Embedding vector dimensionality matches vector index dimension for a query.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is at the embedding-model-to-index contract boundary, not just local math. REAL-INFRA: network/API can matter, but MOCK-HIDE is stronger here: a stubbed embedding client can always return the expected length and miss a real provider/model dimension change. LLM-DEP: this behavior executes a real AI/ML model call; even with deterministic assertion (length), the risk is in real model output contract. MULTI-STEP: single model step, so not Workflow. Adversarial critique: if the system hard-validates model ID->dimension locally and never trusts provider output, Integration could be enough; that disqualifying evidence is not in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model configured for production with a fixed query, then assert returned vector length equals the live index dimension read from vector DB metadata; fail on mismatch.

behaviors.description: Similarity search returns results in descending cosine similarity.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is mostly module handoff (query vector -> search layer -> ordered results). A pure Unit test of sorting can miss wiring bugs (wrong score field, ascending flag, post-processing reorder). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required if using an in-process/local test index. MOCK-HIDE: full mocking of search backend can hide ranking bugs, so use real local search module/index. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Adversarial critique: if using a hosted vector DB with engine-specific ordering semantics, System could be required; behavior text does not require hosted/real network DB.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local test collection with known vectors, run search through service/repository wiring, and assert returned scores are monotonic non-increasing and IDs match expected cosine ranking.

behaviors.description: LLM re-ranker changes ordering for nuanced-intent queries versus raw similarity.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the target failure is the model decision itself (whether nuanced intent causes reorder), not just orchestration. MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed LLM response can trivially force reorder and hide real non-reordering behavior. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real LLM judgment/output quality. MULTI-STEP: one agentic decision point, so Agentic not Workflow. Adversarial critique: if behavior only checked that reranker endpoint is called and output is applied, Integration would suffice; the text explicitly requires semantic reorder on nuanced intent, which disqualifies that demotion.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixed nuanced query and a fixed candidate list where lexical similarity conflicts with intent; invoke real reranker model and assert at least one top-N position changes from raw vector ranking in repeated runs with acceptance threshold.

behaviors.description: No-close-match query returns empty results instead of low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is typically at handoff/thresholding boundary (search scores -> filtering -> API response), so Unit may miss it. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with local seeded vectors and deterministic thresholds; no OS/network necessity. LLM-DEP: behavior can be tested without relying on model judgment by constructing clearly distant vectors/scores; thus Agentic is not required. MULTI-STEP: no agentic chain required. Adversarial critique: if product meaning of "low-relevance noise" is judged by real embedding/LLM semantics in production traffic, Agentic might be needed; behavior text does not require semantic quality evaluation, only empty-set behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Seed test index with vectors, issue a query/vector known to be below relevance threshold for all items, run full search+filter pipeline, and assert HTTP response contains empty results.

behaviors.description: Median end-to-end query latency (ingest -> embed -> search -> rerank -> response) stays under 2s.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: latency failures appear from real HTTP stack, network hops, serialization, and external service timing; in-process fakes understate or hide them. MOCK-HIDE: mocked model/vector DB calls can pass while real deployment breaches SLA. LLM-DEP: assertion is on timing, not output quality; this disqualifies Agentic despite model calls in path. MULTI-STEP: pipeline has multiple stages, but not multiple agentic decision-quality checks, so Workflow is not required. Adversarial critique: if SLO target were explicitly for internal code path excluding network/provider latency, Integration could be enough; behavior text says end-to-end, which disqualifies that.
behaviors.test_description: Run load test against deployed API endpoint with real embedding/vector DB/LLM dependencies for representative median traffic; compute p50 latency and assert < 2000 ms.
